channel.title: Sqwod Daily
channel.description: The fitness industry, decoded — every weekday.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 17 July 2026
item.description: Fresh industry data just dropped — and the gap between operators who benchmark and those who guess is about to get wider.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 17 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Friday, 17 July 2026 · Weekday
Fresh industry data just dropped — and the gap between operators who benchmark and those who guess is about to get wider.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
DSSV drops 2026 DACH fitness industry benchmarks
The DSSV has released its headline figures on the German-speaking fitness market for 2026—giving coaches and operators a fresh data baseline to benchmark growth, justify investment, and spot where the real opportunity is hiding.
Source: DSSV →
02
body LIFE Is Hosting an Exclusive Live Webinar for Your Studio
body LIFE is bringing top expertise straight to studio owners via an exclusive live webinar. Consider this your sign to tune in.
Source: body LIFE →
03
Movement is Therapy — Wolfgang Unsöld Makes the Case
Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's argument that movement isn't just exercise — it's medicine. For coaches and studio owners, it's a timely reminder that the strongest selling point you have might not be aesthetics, but healing.
Source: Trainingsworld →
04
Engadget's Latest Reviews: Steam, Samsung & Oura Tested
Engadget just dropped a fresh batch of reviews covering the Steam Machine, Samsung Music Studio 7, Oura Ring 5, and more — if you're deciding what tech to invest in for your studio or clients, this roundup is a solid starting point.
Source: Engadget →
05
40 Studios and Counting: Pvolve Keeps Signing Franchise Deals
Pvolve has hit 40 open locations and is still inking new franchise agreements — a signal worth watching for anyone sizing up where functional fitness franchises are actually gaining traction.
Source: Athletech News →
06
50 Yards of Data: MacIntyre Brings a Fitness Coach to the Fairway
Bob MacIntyre enlisted a fitness coach to map out 50-yard run-outs on Birkdale's sun-baked turf — proof that on-course performance analytics aren't just for the gym floor anymore, and that your expertise might have a market you haven't pitched yet.
Source: The Scotsman →
Stat
40
Pvolve open franchise locations
Forty open locations and still signing deals signals that functional fitness franchises have cleared the 'concept' phase and entered real scale — worth tracking if you're sizing up the franchise landscape or competing against one moving into your market.
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Monitor your revenue-per-member monthly, not just total revenue — it's the single metric that reveals whether growth is healthy or just busy.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Thursday, 16 July 2026
item.description: The best studios don't just change bodies — they change the story patients tell their doctors.
item.content:encoded:
Thursday, 16 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Thursday, 16 July 2026 · Weekday
The best studios don't just change bodies — they change the story patients tell their doctors.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
DSSV drops the 2026 DACH fitness industry numbers
The DSSV has unveiled the key figures shaping the fitness market across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland for 2026. If you run a gym or coach clients in the region, this is the benchmark data you'll actually want on your radar.
Source: DSSV →
02
body LIFE Is Hosting an Exclusive Webinar for Your Studio
body LIFE is offering top expertise for studio owners through a live, exclusive webinar. Consider this your invite to tune in.
Source: body LIFE →
03
Movement Is Therapy — Wolfgang Unsöld Makes the Case
Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's philosophy that movement isn't just training — it's treatment. For coaches and studio operators, it's a timely reminder that how you frame what you do can be just as powerful as what you actually do.
Source: Trainingsworld →
04
Engadget's Review Recap: Steam Machine, Oura Ring 5 & More
Engadget rounded up reviews of the Steam Machine, Samsung Music Studio 7, Oura Ring 5, and more. Consider this your cheat sheet before you spend a dime.
Source: Engadget →
05
ClassPass Just Got a Little More Studio-Friendly
ClassPass has rolled out new policies and protections designed with studios in mind — which means if you've been side-eyeing the platform, it might be worth a second look.
Source: Athletech News →
06
50 Yards of Data: MacIntyre's Coach Maps Birkdale's Baked Terrain
Bob MacIntyre is using a fitness coach to calculate 50-yard run-outs on sun-hardened Birkdale fairways — proof that course management is now as much a conditioning discipline as a caddie conversation.
Source: The Scotsman →
Policy watch
ClassPass adds studio-side protections
ClassPass has rolled out new policies designed with studio operators in mind. If rate dilution or booking control kept you off the platform, the updated terms are worth a fresh read before you dismiss the channel again.
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Monitor your studio's medical-referral conversion rate as a standalone KPI — the ratio of clients who came via a physio, GP, or allied-health professional. Even a small uptick signals your therapeutic positioning is landing with the right referrers.
✓ Steal: Build a one-page 'clinical translation sheet' that maps each class or service to a common movement complaint (back pain, post-surgical rehab, chronic fatigue) — hand it to local physios as a referral tool, not a sales brochure.
✓ Try: Run a 30-day intake-language experiment: split new clients into two onboarding email versions — one outcome-framed ('restore function'), one feature-framed ('our class types') — and compare 60-day retention between cohorts.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Wednesday, 15 July 2026
item.description: The data nerds have spoken — Europe's fitness numbers just dropped, and if you're not benchmarking, you're just guessing.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday, 15 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Wednesday, 15 July 2026 · Weekday
The data nerds have spoken — Europe's fitness numbers just dropped, and if you're not benchmarking, you're just guessing.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
DSSV drops 2026 DACH fitness industry benchmarks
The DSSV has released its key figures for the DACH fitness market in 2026, giving operators a fresh data baseline to benchmark performance. If you're setting targets or pitching investors this year, this is the number-crunching you actually want on your desk.
Source: DSSV →
02
body LIFE locks in top studio expertise for live webinar
Industry insiders are bringing hard-won studio know-how straight to your screen in an exclusive live session. If you run a gym or coach clients, this is the shortcut to insights that usually cost a consulting invoice.
Source: body LIFE →
03
Garage Gym vs. Membership: Torque Fitness Ideas Explored
มูลนิธิเพื่อผู้บริโภค มพบ. published a look at garage gym ideas featuring treadmills and Torque Fitness, alongside the pros of owning a home setup versus holding a gym membership. Consider this your nudge to finally clear out those moving boxes.
Source: มูลนิธิเพื่อผู้บริโภค มพบ. →
04
CorePower Yoga Swaps CEOs, Eyes Studio Expansion
CorePower Yoga has made a CEO change and is signaling a push toward studio growth, per Athletech News. Looks like someone's got a new warrior pose for the boardroom.
Source: Athletech News →
05
Lars Springer Is European Champion — and Trainingsworld Has the Story
Lars Springer has claimed the European Championship title, and Trainingsworld is your source for the details. Worth a read if you're looking for proof that the training grind actually pays off.
Source: Trainingsworld →
Policy watch
Consumer advocates are shaping gym-vs-home decisions before you get a word in
Thailand's Foundation for Consumers is actively weighing in on the garage gym vs. membership debate, meaning prospective members may arrive at your door pre-loaded with advocacy-group talking points. Operators should know what arguments are circulating so they can address them proactively in their sales and onboarding conversations.
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Start monitoring your revenue-per-active-member monthly — not just total revenue. It surfaces pricing and retention problems that top-line numbers hide until it's too late.
✓ Steal: Run a 15-minute 'competitor audit' each quarter: visit one rival studio as a mystery shopper and score their onboarding experience against your own. Cheap intel, high ROI.
✓ Try: Before your next leadership hire or strategic pivot, write a one-page 'operator thesis' — what you believe about your market that others don't. It sharpens decisions and doubles as investor narrative.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 14 July 2026
item.description: The scoreboard just dropped — and if you haven't set your 2026 targets yet, DACH's new industry numbers are the excuse you needed.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 14 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Tuesday, 14 July 2026 · Weekday
The scoreboard just dropped — and if you haven't set your 2026 targets yet, DACH's new industry numbers are the excuse you needed.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
DSSV drops 2026 DACH fitness industry benchmarks
The DSSV has unveiled the key figures shaping the fitness market across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland for 2026. If you run a gym or coach clients, these numbers are the scoreboard you'll want on your wall before setting this year's targets.
Source: DSSV →
02
Garage Gym vs. Membership: The Treadmill Dilemma Is Real
Consumerthai.org breaks down the honest pros of owning a garage gym — treadmill and all — versus holding a membership. For operators, it's a useful reminder that your competition isn't just the gym across the street anymore.
Source: consumerthai.org →
03
Lars Springer Is Your New European Champion 🏆
Trainingsworld reports that Lars Springer has claimed the European Championship title. Consider this your cue to Google him and feel simultaneously inspired and unqualified.
Source: Trainingsworld →
04
Engadget's Latest Reviews: Steam Machine, Oura Ring 5 & More
Engadget just dropped a fresh review roundup covering the Steam Machine, Samsung Music Studio 7, Oura Ring 5, and more — if you're advising clients on wearables or studio tech, this is your weekend reading.
Source: Engadget →
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Start logging member visit frequency weekly — not monthly. Operators who catch a drop in visit cadence early retain members who would have quietly churned in 30 days.
✓ Steal: Run a quarterly 'competitor audit': sign up for a trial at one direct competitor and one non-obvious alternative (online app, home-gym community) to map their onboarding experience against yours.
✓ Try: Use a single post-cancel survey question — 'What will you do instead?' — to surface your real competitors. The answers will surprise you and sharpen your retention messaging immediately.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Monday, 13 July 2026
item.description: The session that feels like a freebie might actually be your highest-converting sales call — if you run it right.
item.content:encoded:
Monday, 13 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Monday, 13 July 2026 · Weekday
The session that feels like a freebie might actually be your highest-converting sales call — if you run it right.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
DSSV drops the 2026 DACH fitness industry numbers
The DSSV has released its headline figures for the fitness industry across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland for 2026 — giving studio operators and coaches a fresh benchmarking baseline to see exactly where they stand in the market.
Source: DSSV →
02
body LIFE webinar brings studio-growth experts to your screen
body LIFE is locking an exclusive live webinar packed with top-tier industry expertise — the kind of strategic firepower that usually costs a conference ticket and a red-eye flight. For coaches and operators, that means actionable insight you can plug into your business the same week.
Source: body LIFE →
03
Garage Gym vs. Membership: The Treadmill Dilemma Is Real
Consumerthai.org breaks down the honest pros of owning a garage gym — including Torque Fitness setups — versus holding a membership. For studio operators, it's a useful reminder that convenience and ownership are the two biggest arguments you're competing against every single day.
Source: consumerthai.org →
04
Movement Assessments Are a Revenue Tool — Here's the Proof
Athletech News breaks down how movement assessments do more than catch faulty squats — they create a structured upsell moment that turns a first session into a long-term client relationship.
Source: Athletech News →
05
Lars Springer Is European Champion — Here's What That Means
Trainingsworld is spotlighting Lars Springer's European Championship win, and for coaches and studio operators, stories like this are gold: real athletes, real results, and exactly the kind of social proof that fills your next training cycle's waitlist.
Source: Trainingsworld →
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Monitor your intro-session-to-paid-package conversion rate weekly — if you don't have a number today, that's the problem. Even a rough tally in a spreadsheet will show you which coach or format closes best within 30 days.
✓ Steal: Borrow the 'before photo equivalent' principle from personal training: give new clients a written baseline score after their first session. People protect progress they can see on paper — it's a retention tool disguised as admin.
✓ Try: Run a 15-minute 'studio economics' review each quarter: compare your revenue-per-square-metre against your programming schedule. Dead floor space during off-peak hours is usually the fastest untapped margin in a small studio.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
AOL.com went full Consumer Reports on gym memberships and somehow the YMCA came out swinging — which means somewhere a spin studio founder just refreshed their pricing page in a cold sweat.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 10 July 2026
item.description: The numbers are in — and if you're running a DACH gym without them, you're pricing, staffing, and expanding in the dark.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 10 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Friday, 10 July 2026 · Weekday
The numbers are in — and if you're running a DACH gym without them, you're pricing, staffing, and expanding in the dark.
— Sqwod
Move of the Day
A unilateral power circuit that hunts down imbalances — 40 on / 20 off, core working overtime.
Coach: @its__kay.kay · InstagramWatch →
The rundown
01
DSSV drops the 2026 DACH fitness industry numbers
The DSSV has unveiled the headline figures shaping the German-speaking fitness market in 2026. If you run a gym or coach clients in the region, this is the benchmark data you'll want on your radar before making any business moves this year.
Source: DSSV →
02
Garmin's mystery 'CIRQA' trademark could mean a new sensor is coming
Garmin has filed CIRQA trademarks in both Europe and Canada, strongly hinting at an unannounced sensor product. For coaches and operators already deep in the wearables ecosystem, it's worth keeping an eye on what Garmin's cooking up next.
Source: Notebookcheck →
03
A running coach's picks for the best fitness trackers in 2026
The Independent tapped a running coach to cut through the wearable noise and name the best fitness trackers heading into 2026. If your clients are asking what to strap on their wrists, now you've got a credible answer to send them.
Source: the-independent.com →
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Monitor your revenue-per-active-member monthly, not just total revenue — it's the single ratio that exposes whether growth is real or just volume masking churn.
✓ Steal: Offer a 10-minute 'results audit' call to clients every 90 days: it increases retention, surfaces upsell moments, and costs you nothing but a calendar slot.
✓ Try: Run a one-question post-class survey ('What would make your next session 10% better?') — the qualitative signal you get beats any NPS score for actionable programming tweaks.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Thursday, 09 July 2026
item.description: The best marketing budget you're not spending is already sitting in your group chat.
item.content:encoded:
Thursday, 09 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Thursday, 09 July 2026 · Weekday
The best marketing budget you're not spending is already sitting in your group chat.
— Sqwod
Move of the Day
Seven moves for elite single-leg strength — in-game, the body spends most of its time on one leg.
Coach: @realgame.athletics · InstagramWatch →
Connect the dots
Your members will sell for you — if you let them
There's a reason cult studios outgrow their square footage before they outgrow their waiting lists: belonging is stickier than any promo code. The operators winning right now aren't just programming great classes — they're designing moments that make members feel like insiders, then stepping back while those members do the recruiting.
So what does that look like in practice? Referral loops built around identity, not incentives. A member who says 'this is my community' will bring three friends; a member chasing a free class brings one, maybe. Audit your touchpoints this week — onboarding, milestones, social proof — and ask whether each one deepens belonging or just delivers a service. The ones that deepen it are your real marketing budget.
The rundown
01
The DACH fitness industry is still growing, per new 2026 data
The DSSV's latest DACH study confirms the fitness industry is continuing its upward trajectory into 2026 — which means the market is still expanding, and operators who move now are swimming with the current, not against it.
Source: DSSV →
02
body LIFE Is Hosting a Protected Webinar Packed With Studio Expertise
body LIFE has announced an exclusive live webinar promising top-tier expertise for studio operators. The session is described as protected, so you'll want to secure your spot before the doors close.
Source: body LIFE →
03
Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld Has the Receipts
Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's case for movement as a therapeutic tool — a timely reminder that coaches who can articulate the *why* behind training don't just retain clients, they become indispensable to them.
Source: Trainingsworld →
04
WHOOP taps former Nike exec as its new Chief Marketing Officer
Fitness wearable brand WHOOP has named Dirk-Jan van Hameren, a former Nike executive, as its Chief Marketing Officer. The appointment signals a big-brand marketing push for the performance-focused wearable.
Source: Storyboard18 →
05
Your Community Is a Marketing Engine — Are You Using It?
Fitness and wellness brands are waking up to something coaches have known forever: people don't just buy a workout, they buy a sense of belonging. According to Athletech News, smart operators are now building that tribe intentionally — and letting it do the heavy marketing lifting for them.
Source: Athletech News →
06
Vaura Pilates Hits 10 Studios With a Jakarta Grand Opening
The reformer brand just planted its tenth flag in Jakarta, Indonesia — proof that boutique Pilates is still finding new floors to roll out on. For studio operators eyeing international expansion, Vaura's trajectory is worth a close watch.
Source: The Globe and Mail →
Stat
10
Vaura Pilates studio locations after Jakarta grand opening
Boutique Pilates is still finding new markets to enter — a useful reality check for operators who think reformer saturation is already here.
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Start measuring your referral rate as a standalone KPI — divide new members who came via existing-member referral by total new members each month. If it's under 20%, your community isn't doing its job yet.
✓ Steal: Borrow the 'milestone post' playbook from SaaS communities: publicly celebrate member anniversaries, PRs, and transformations inside your owned channels to create social proof loops that compound without ad spend.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Wednesday, 08 July 2026
item.description: You're already building the tribe — you're just not billing it as a growth strategy yet.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday, 08 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Wednesday, 08 July 2026 · Weekday
You're already building the tribe — you're just not billing it as a growth strategy yet.
— Sqwod
Move of the Day
Seven iso-dynamic core crushers — abs and obliques trained for stability, reactivity, and rotation.
Coach: @realgame.athletics · InstagramWatch →
The rundown
01
DSSV drops the 2026 DACH fitness industry numbers
The DSSV has unveiled the key benchmarks shaping the German-speaking fitness market in 2026—giving coaches and operators a fresh data baseline to stress-test pricing, membership targets, and growth plans before the year really heats up.
Source: DSSV →
02
body LIFE locks in top studio expertise for exclusive live webinar
Industry heavyweights are bringing their A-game to a protected, invite-only session — if you run a studio, this is the room you actually want to be in.
Source: body LIFE →
03
Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld Has the Receipts
Trainingsworld's latest deep-dive profiles Wolfgang Unsöld's case for movement as a therapeutic tool — a timely reminder for coaches that the best pitch for your services might not be aesthetics or performance, but plain old healing.
Source: Trainingsworld →
04
Loop Air Is the Budget Fitness Tracker Gunning for Whoop
Notebookcheck spotlights the Loop Air, a wallet-friendly alternative to Whoop that still packs GPS and ECG. Your wrist's getting fancy without your bank account noticing.
Source: Notebookcheck →
05
Community Is the New Marketing Budget
Fitness and wellness brands are quietly turning their communities into full-blown marketing engines — meaning the tribe you're already building around your studio or coaching practice isn't just good culture, it's your most underleveraged growth channel.
Source: Athletech News →
06
Gold Tries a Comeback—But Trend Walls Are Blocking the Path
FXEmpire's latest XAU/USD forecast shows gold mounting a recovery attempt that's running straight into major trend resistance. Whether the bulls can break through remains the big open question.
Source: FXEmpire →
Sqwod recs
✓ Steal: Run a 'founding member' wall — physical or digital — that publicly names your longest-tenured clients. Recognition triggers loyalty and signals social proof to every prospect who walks in or lands on your site.
✓ Try: Use a free NPS-style check-in (one question: 'How likely are you to recommend us?') sent via SMS after a client's first 30 days. The replies will tell you exactly who your potential community ambassadors are before you ever have to ask.
✓ Track: Start measuring referral source for every new member signup — even a simple dropdown in your intake form. Without this baseline, you'll never know whether your community efforts are actually converting, and you'll keep over-investing in paid channels by default.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 07 July 2026
item.description: Before you renew that Meta ad spend, check what's already growing for free in your group chats.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 07 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Tuesday, 07 July 2026 · Weekday
Before you renew that Meta ad spend, check what's already growing for free in your group chats.
— Sqwod
Move of the Day
Rotation isn't filler, it's a pillar — Vernon Griffith on building rotational work into any athlete's program.
Coach: @vernongriffith4 · InstagramWatch →
The rundown
01
DSSV drops the 2026 DACH fitness industry benchmarks
The industry body has published the current key figures for the fitness market in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland — the numbers every studio operator and trainer will be measured against this year.
Source: DSSV →
02
body LIFE beams top studio expertise into a live webinar
Trade magazine body LIFE is inviting studio operators to an exclusive live webinar — real, immediately applicable business know-how. If you want to level up your studio, block the date.
Source: body LIFE →
03
Loop Air brings GPS and ECG to budget wearables
A new Whoop alternative called Loop Air is offering GPS and ECG tracking at a lower price point — worth a look if your clients want data-rich recovery tools without the premium subscription sting.
Source: Notebookcheck →
04
Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld Has the Receipts
Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's case for movement as a therapeutic tool — a timely reminder for coaches that the science backs what you've been selling all along.
Source: Trainingsworld →
05
Community Is the New Marketing Budget
Fitness and wellness brands are ditching traditional ads and leaning on their communities as a full-blown growth engine — which means the tribe you've been building in your studio might be your most underrated business asset.
Source: Athletech News →
06
Garmin Forerunner 70 Hits India With a Price Tag and Specs
Garmin has launched the Forerunner 70 in India, and TelecomTalk has the full breakdown on what it costs and what it does.
Source: TelecomTalk →
Sqwod recs
✓ Steal: Run a 'bring a friend free for a week' window tied to an existing challenge or milestone — referrals spike when there's a natural social moment to attach them to, not just an open invitation.
✓ Track: Start measuring Net Promoter Score monthly with a single post-class text: 'How likely are you to recommend us to a friend, 0–10?' Trends in that number predict churn and word-of-mouth growth before you see either on your P&L.
✓ Try: Create one member-only touchpoint that has nothing to do with training — a monthly coffee, a Spotify playlist they help curate, a members-only Q&A — something that builds identity around belonging, not just workouts.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Monday, 06 July 2026
item.description: Plunges, pads, and boutique content drops — today's fitness business is being built in the spaces between the reps.
item.content:encoded:
Monday, 06 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Monday, 06 July 2026 · Weekday
Plunges, pads, and boutique content drops — today's fitness business is being built in the spaces between the reps.
— Sqwod
Money movement
▲  Raise
Heatronics
 Rs 1.8 crore (₹18M)
seed round led by Inflection Point Ventures to scale wellness hardware
The rundown
01
Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Officially a Wellness Industry Talking Point
The Global Wellness Institute is putting contrast hydrotherapy on the map — meaning your clients are about to start asking about cold plunges and hot soaks whether you're ready or not. Now's a good time to have an answer.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Rhone Is Betting Big on IRL — Stores, Events, the Works
The activewear brand's CEO says in-person shopping and live experiences are a core growth play, not an afterthought. For studio owners and operators, that's a timely reminder that the physical floor still sells.
Source: Athletech News →
03
Fitness On Demand Just Got a Boutique Studio Upgrade
Fitness On Demand is teaming up with JETSWEAT to bring more boutique studio content to its platform. For operators looking to keep members sweating without building a content library from scratch, this one's worth a look.
Source: Health & Fitness Association →
04
Rs 1.8 Cr Backs Heatronics to Heat Up the Wellness Device Space
Inflection Point Ventures led the seed round for wellness hardware startup Heatronics — a signal that investors are warming up to recovery and device-led fitness beyond just apps. If you're a studio operator eyeing hardware partnerships, the funding pipeline just got a little more interesting.
Source: Indian Startup Times →
05
Heatronics Pulls In Rs 1.8 Crore for Wellness Devices
The Indian wellness tech firm just closed a Rs 1.8 crore raise to scale its hardware business — a reminder that the market for recovery and performance devices has real investment heat behind it, not just consumer hype.
Source: Siliconindia →
06
Movement is Medicine: Wolfgang Unsöld Makes the Case
Trainingsworld's latest argues that movement isn't just exercise — it's therapy. For coaches looking to deepen client buy-in, the science-backed framing is a ready-made conversation starter.
Source: Trainingsworld →
Stat
Rs 1.8 crore
seed funding raised by Heatronics
It's a small number globally, but it signals that investors in emerging markets are writing real checks for recovery hardware — the category is attracting capital, not just consumer curiosity.
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Monitor your front-desk cancel and pause reasons for 90 days — most operators discover that 'life got busy' is code for 'I stopped feeling results,' which is exactly the gap a recovery offering closes.
✓ Steal: Build a 3-email welcome sequence that explicitly teaches new members how to use your space between classes — stretching cues, recovery tips, hydration prompts. Retention starts at orientation, not at month three.
✓ Try: Run a single 'themed open house' event in your studio — invite members to bring one guest and attach it to a specific skill, modality, or topic night. In-person community moments drive referrals more reliably than discount codes.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Rhone is opening more stores because apparently 'just buy it online' wasn't the whole answer — a lesson the fitness industry learned the hard way and then forgot, and is now learning again.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 03 July 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 03 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Friday, 03 July 2026 · Weekday
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Money movement
▲  Raise
Heatronics
 Rs 1.8 crore (₹18M)
seed round led by Inflection Point Ventures to scale heat-based wellness devices
The rundown
01
Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Having a Moment — Here's Why
The Global Wellness Institute is putting contrast hydrotherapy on the radar, and for coaches and studio operators, that means one thing: your clients are about to start asking questions. Get ahead of it.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Rhone Is Betting Big on Brick-and-Mortar and IRL Events
The activewear brand's CEO says they're doubling down on in-person shopping and events — a reminder that for fitness-adjacent brands, the gym floor (and the store floor) still beats the scroll.
Source: Athletech News →
03
Fitness On Demand Just Got a Boutique Upgrade
Fitness On Demand has partnered with JETSWEAT to bring more boutique studio content to its platform — meaning operators can now offer members that curated, high-energy class feel without needing to actually build out the studio.
Source: Health & Fitness Association →
04
Rs 1.8 Crore Bet: Heatronics Is Heating Up the Wellness Device Space
Indian startup Heatronics just closed a Rs 1.8 crore raise to scale its wellness device business. If you're watching the hardware side of recovery and performance tech, this one's worth keeping on your radar.
Source: Siliconindia →
05
Heatronics Heats Up With Rs 1.8 Cr Seed Round
Wellness device startup Heatronics has raised Rs 1.8 crore in seed funding led by Inflection Point Ventures. The fresh capital will fuel expansion of its wellness device business.
Source: Indian Startup Times →
06
Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld Has the Receipts
Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's case for exercise as a genuine therapeutic tool — a timely reminder for coaches and studio owners that the science behind what you do is your strongest sales pitch.
Source: Trainingsworld →
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Monitor your revenue-per-square-foot monthly — not just total revenue. It forces honest questions about which physical spaces are earning their keep and which are just costing you lease.
✓ Steal: Build a one-page 'science card' for your top three signature modalities — one study, one plain-English sentence, one client outcome. Post it at the relevant station. Turns your methodology into credibility without a single paid ad.
✓ Try: Run a 60-minute 'founder office hours' session inside your studio once a month — open to members, no agenda. The intel you get on what they actually want is worth more than most member surveys.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Wolfgang Unsöld is out here using peer-reviewed science as a sales pitch, which is either the future of fitness marketing or the world's longest SOAP note.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Thursday, 02 July 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Thursday, 02 July 2026 View online →
DAILY
The fitness industry, decoded — in five minutes.
Thursday, 02 July 2026 · Weekday
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
Precision is the new premium in fitness
Three things landed this week that look unrelated — a longevity window for strength, a women's-only lifting studio, and a hydrotherapy revival — but they're all saying the same thing: vague wellness is losing, specificity is winning. Clients don't want 'move more'; they want a protocol, a number, a room designed exactly for them. The operators building around precise outcomes are the ones capturing boutique dollars right now.
This is your competitive moat. When the Global Wellness Institute publishes a tidy strength window and a studio concept launches around a single demographic's lifting identity, the market is signaling that operators who can answer 'why this, how much, for whom' will out-retain and out-acquire everyone still selling general fitness vibes. Precision isn't a niche play anymore — it's the product.
The rundown
01
Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Having a Moment — Here's Why
The Global Wellness Institute is shining a spotlight on contrast hydrotherapy as a rising recovery modality. If you're not already talking hot-cold protocols with your clients, now's a good time to warm up to the idea.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Founders Row launches LiftHer, a women's strength studio
Founders Row is entering the women's strength space with its new LiftHer studio concept — a signal that dedicated female lifting environments are moving from niche to next big thing for operators watching where boutique dollars are flowing.
Source: Athletech News →
03
The Best Fitness & Wellness Launches This Season, Ranked
From the Oura Ring 5 to an AI-powered scented sleep gadget, Athletech News rounded up this season's most notable fitness and wellness drops — worth a scan if you're advising clients on gear or scouting what's actually cutting through the noise.
Source: Athletech News →
04
The Weekly Workout Sweet Spot for a Longer Life? 90–119 Mins
A new study covered by the Global Wellness Institute found that 90–119 minutes of strength training per week may be the magic window for longevity. Not too little, not too much — just right, says the science.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
Stat
90–119 min/week
Weekly strength training for maximum longevity impact
This is the number to build programming blocks around — not because it's a round target, but because it's specific enough to hand a client and watch their commitment sharpen. Ranges outperform vague minimums for adherence.
Sqwod recs
✓ Track: Start logging client retention by format type (strength vs. cardio vs. recovery) — if you don't know which modality retains longest at your studio, you're pricing and scheduling on instinct, not signal.
✓ Steal: Borrow the 'designed for one demographic' brief from specialty studio concepts: pick your highest-LTV client segment and write a one-page studio-within-a-studio concept for them — even if you never build it, the exercise sharpens your positioning.
✓ Try: Run a 10-minute contrast protocol (hot finish followed by a cold rinse) as an optional add-on after one weekly class, track NPS separately for those who try it vs. those who don't — cheap way to validate recovery demand before investing in hardware.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Somewhere out there, a gadget that pumps scented air at you while you sleep is being called a wellness launch of the season. The bar for 'cutting through the noise' has never been more fragrant.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know someone who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Forward to a friend →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
